question: Among Bexar, El Paso, Hidalgo, and Montgomery counties, which have population under 900,000 in the census of two years before the year (within 2015-2023) that has the highest total number of drug overdose deaths across U.S. states using December 12 month-ending values and excluding the U.S. total and are not in the Houston metropolitan area? List the county names in alphabetical order separated by `, `. Your response should be in the format [Answer].

1.fact: In the VSRR provisional drug overdose death counts dataset (December 12 month-ending values for 2015-2023, excluding the US aggregate), 2022 has the highest total across states at 109,413.
1.subquestion: Which year from 2015-2023 has the highest total number of drug overdose deaths across U.S. states, using December 12 month-ending values and excluding the U.S. total? (Filter Indicator == "Number of Drug Overdose Deaths", Period == "12 month-ending", Month == "December", exclude State == "US"; sum Data Value by Year; choose the maximum.)

2.fact: According to the <node_1 answer - 2> Census Redistricting Data for Texas counties, among {Bexar, El Paso, Hidalgo, Montgomery}: El Paso had population 865,657, Hidalgo had 870,781, and Montgomery had 620,443 (all under 900,000), while Bexar had 2,009,324 (over 900,000).
2.subquestion: Among Bexar, El Paso, Hidalgo, and Montgomery counties, which have <node_1 answer - 2> census population under 900,000?

3.fact: According to the Wikipedia article for Montgomery County, Texas, "Montgomery County is part of the Houston–The Woodlands–Sugar Land metropolitan statistical area."
3.subquestion: Among <node_2 answer>, which counties are NOT in the Houston metropolitan area?

reasoning_chain: Node 1: Year with highest drug overdose deaths (2015-2023) -> 2022
